chunk_details.text: energy at all and a diminishing enthusiasm for life. Asked to what she attributed her weight gain, ill health, and depression, she offered another one-word answer: “Menopause.” After five years of decline, she caught the flu just as winter set in, and it knocked her down completely. Although the infection was eventually zapped by rest, fluids, and symptom-controlling medicines, Diana remained ill throughout the winter. Even spring offered no jump of resilience to snap her back. All the symptoms that had bothered her before the flu now simply seemed worse, and she despaired of ever feeling well again. The straw that broke the camel’s back and sent Diana to a functional medicine practitioner was that her hair started falling out. Fast. She couldn’t help her reaction: Stupefied at finding herself clutching a handful of hair, she burst into tears. “What is happening to me?” she sobbed, “and why can’t someone fix it?” Diana sought out one of the physicians in our group at the Functional Medicine Clinical Research Center here in Seattle and was given a full functional medicine evaluation. The conclusion was that she had a significant imbalance in her detoxification and defense processes and needed a personalized program that would be layered onto the diet and dietary supplement components of the baseline program to address those core imbalances. Of particular importance also was the mindfulness-focused cognitive behavioral training; this was key to managing the stress that both Diana and her functional medicine practitioner felt was a significant contributor to her core imbalances. Here are some of the modifications in Diana’s personalized plan. • A low-allergy food plan with increased vegetable protein—plus a rice protein supplement—and increased levels of vegetables, especially of the cruciferous family, containing phytonutrients that specifically support detoxification • Increased water intake of at least six 8-ounce glasses per day • A supplement of sodium bicarbonate daily to augment alkaline reserve • An inulin prebiotic and a high-potency probiotic • A tailored nutritional supplement program delivering specified levels of vitamins, minerals, and phytonutrients to support the detoxification process, including: A balanced B-vitamin complement with increased amounts of folic acid, vitamin B12, pantothenic acid, biotin, and the trace mineral zinc A phytonutrient supplement composed of concentrates from broccoli and brussels sprouts as well as a supplement of N- acetylcysteine to support glutathione needs for the detoxification process Diana saw and felt little progress in the first month of this program, and she questioned whether it was worth the effort. By the sixth week, however, she began to realize that she was feeling better; above all, she noticed that the hair loss had stopped and that new hair was growing back into places where it had thinned considerably. This was sufficient encouragement for her to stick with her program; by twelve weeks she could record enough progress that she felt and looked like a “new woman”—her phrase. She felt so much better, in fact, that she adhered to the program for six months in all. By then, she had lost more than thirty-five pounds, was taking only two daily medications, had a full head of hair, and was wearing clothes she had not worn for seven years. Her cloud of depression had also lifted; Diana felt a renewed zest for living. She and her physician now adjusted her personalized health- management program to reflect her new condition, giving her greater latitude in her food choices and a lower level of nutritional supplementation. That was six years ago as I write this, and Diana remains a woman of high energy and great stamina, powers present in her genetic makeup that had been dampened by the specifics of her gene-environment interaction and that were unlocked by her personalized health management intervention and her ongoing health-management program. No book can evaluate you and pinpoint your most significant process imbalance, as her physician did for Diana. No book can design a personalized health-management program for you to the level of detail of Diana’s program. What this book can do—and what we will do in this chapter—is provide planning tools that can help shape your analysis so you can draw the right insights from your own observations and self-assessments. Just as you look for patterns in which you observe and assess, this chapter shows you some of the patterns typical of imbalances in each of the seven core physiological processes—and it suggests ways to restore balance to each system, and therefore to the body’s systems network as a whole. A more detailed guide to additional resources—professional help from a practitioner trained in functional medicine, for example, or access to coaching, support, or additional information—will be provided in Appendix C, “Resources.” One of the tools that you may want to avail yourself of—and one that will be further discussed in Appendix C—is genetic testing,

question: ketoziste olduğumu nasıl anlarım
answer:
Merhaba, nasılsınız? Ketoziste olup olmadığınızı anlamak için kaynaklarda doğrudan bir yöntem belirtilmemiş, ancak ketozis durumunda vücudunuzda meydana gelen bazı değişiklikler ve belirtiler hakkında bilgiler mevcut.

Şöyle ki, ketojenik diyet uyguladığınızda vücudunuzun ana enerji kaynağı olarak glikoz yerine yağ yakmaya başlamasıyla ketozis adı verilen bir duruma girilir. Bu durumda vücut, yağlardan türetilen ketonları yakıt olarak kullanır.

Kaynaklarda belirtilen bazı durumlar ve belirtiler şunlar olabilir:

Vücudunuzun yağ yakmaya başlaması: Ketojenik diyetin amacı, vücudun şeker rezervlerini tüketerek enerji için yağ yakmaya başlamasını sağlamaktır. Bu da keton adı verilen moleküllerin üretimine yol açar.
Kilo kaybı: Ketojenik diyetin metabolizmayı hızlandırarak ve iştahı azaltarak kilo kaybını destekleyebileceği belirtilmiştir.
Zihinsel odaklanma, hafıza ve enerji artışı: Bazı kişiler keto diyeti sırasında zihinsel odaklanmada, hafızada ve enerjide iyileşmeler yaşadıklarını bildirmişlerdir.
Nefeste değişiklik: Vücut ketozise girdiğinde, keton adı verilen yan ürünler üretmeye başlar. Bunlardan biri asetondur ve nefes yoluyla vücuttan atılır. Bu durum, nefeste belirgin bir kokuya neden olabilir.
İdrarda keton seviyeleri: Kaynaklarda, sık idrara çıkma, aşırı susuzluk, yüksek kan şekeri seviyeleri ve idrarda yüksek keton seviyeleri gibi belirtilerin Diyabetik Ketoasidoz (DKA) semptomları olabileceği ve bu durumda bir doktora görünmeniz gerektiği belirtilmiştir. Ancak bu, normal ketozis durumunu belirlemek için bir yöntem olarak değil, ciddi bir uyarı olarak sunulmuştur.

Özetle, ketoziste olup olmadığınızı kesin olarak belirlemek için kaynaklarda doğrudan bir test veya yöntemden bahsedilmiyor. Ancak vücudunuzun enerji kaynağını değiştirmesi, kilo kaybı, zihinsel açıklık ve nefes kokusundaki değişiklikler gibi belirtiler bu duruma işaret edebilir. Eğer endişeleriniz varsa veya belirtileriniz şiddetlenirse, mutlaka bir sağlık uzmanına danışmanız en doğrusu olacaktır.

chunk_details.text: are relatively high for an adult (5 to 6 mmol/L or higher), drink two tablespoons of apple or orange juice as well as ½ teaspoon of baking soda in a glass of water. Repeat in 15 minutes if necessary. You may be reluctant to do this because you believe that it will slow your transition to ketosis, but understand that the negative impact of over-ketosis far outweighs any benefits. Please don’t rely on your gut instincts here. Call your doctor or go to the emergency department if your symptoms don’t resolve quickly. A simple blood test including a check of your CO 2 levels will determine if you are acidotic or simply experiencing some transition symptoms. If you are the parent of a young child who is starting this diet, watch for the symptoms noted previously. Acidosis is more common in children, but also easily reversed. Still, err on the side of caution and call your child’s pediatrician for advice if symptoms persist or seem to be worsening. Dizziness, Light-headedness, or Shakiness These symptoms may indicate hypoglycemia. However, these symptoms may also be attributable to a drop in your blood pressure as you eliminate water. Don’t put yourself in a risky situation that requires good balance or a sudden increase in effort, such as climbing a ladder or working out with weights, until you are symptom-free. If the feeling persists more than a few days, have your health care practitioner check your blood pressure. If it’s normal, then your symptoms may be due to mild hypoglycemia (which will resolve quickly once you are in ketosis) or to the brain needing a bit more time to adapt to using ketones instead of glucose for energy. Constipation Most people have fewer bowel movements on a ketogenic diet than they may have had previously. This decrease is normal and does not qualify as constipation (which is straining to pass hard stool). True constipation, however, is such a common issue that I’ve devoted a whole section to it in chapter 15 . For now, be sure that you’re drinking plenty of water and replacing sodium by salting your foods (unless you’ve been told by your doctor to limit sodium intake). You may also want to carefully monitor your fiber intake and add some supplemental fiber, such as ground psyllium seed husk or ground flaxseed. Certain forms of magnesium can also be a huge help; learn about them in chapter 14 before you start the transition to a ketogenic diet. Muscle Aches, Cramping, Weakness, and General Fatigue Some of these sensations can be attributed to the energy gap as your body adjusts to burning fat instead of glucose as its primary fuel. Or they can be one of many symptoms associated with dehydration and/or loss of electrolytes. Salted homemade broth can replace both. If you don’t have broth handy, you can use bouillon (no MSG, please!) or even salted water. (Salted water replaces sodium and chloride but none of the other electrolytes unless you use Morton Lite salt, which contains potassium as well). Also be sure to get enough magnesium. Headache A headache is yet another symptom of dehydration. It may persist for a while, even if you are careful to rehydrate. A cup of salted homemade broth along with ensuring adequate hydration overall will go a long way in resolving this. (Remember, too, that you are not to drink alcohol as you are adapting to the diet.) Change in Blood Pressure If you have a heart condition or high blood pressure, speak with the doctor who manages your condition before you begin the diet, especially if you take medication. Your blood pressure is likely to drop soon after you make the shift to ketosis, and while lower blood pressure is a welcome bonus for many people, it may drop too low if the medication is not adjusted appropriately. Track your blood pressure and talk to your doctor about possibly reducing your dosage. Heart Rate or Rhythm Changes, Including Palpitations A change in blood volume brought on by dehydration and loss of electrolytes, including sodium and potassium, may cause your heart to beat faster and/or harder. Salted broth and proper hydration help to replenish these losses. If you have pre-existing heart disease or rhythm issues (such as atrial fibrillation), dehydration and electrolyte imbalance can possibly trigger an episode. Please discuss this with your doctor before starting this diet. Also, if you have ever received any drugs with prolonged QT interval as a possible side effect, you absolutely need to rule out this condition before deciding to start a ketogenic diet. Change in Exercise Tolerance or Physical Performance Given the many adjustments your body will need to make as you adapt to the diet, it’s best to limit your physical activities until you are mostly keto-adapted

vеgеtаblеѕ аnd рѕуllіum ѕееd huѕkѕ. Sеrіоuѕlу, eat fiber -rich vеggіеѕ аnd рѕуllіum. Thіѕ wіll mаkе your trірѕ tо thе bаt hrооm muсh lеѕѕ раіnful and far mоrе рrоduсtіvе. 20 If уоu еxреrіеnсе dіаrrhеа durіng уоur dіеt, уоu might need to аdd рrоbіоtісѕ to your meals, аlоng with fеrmеntеd fооdѕ like sauerkraut, рісklеѕ, kimchi, tеmреh, аnd miso. Whіlе уоu’rе at іt, be prepared f or a slower mеtаbоlіѕm and stinky brеаth. Keto Dіеt Wаrnіngѕ and Conclusion If уоu’rе on thе kеtо diet and you еxреrіеnсе frequent urіnаtіоn, еxtrеmе thіrѕt, hіgh blood ѕugаr levels, and hіgh levels of keynotes іn thе urіnе, please ѕее a dосtоr or go tо an еmеrgеnсу rооm. Thеѕе are 21 ѕуmрtоmѕ of DKA, оthеrwіѕе knоwn аѕ Dіаbеtіс Ketoacidosis. Mоѕt соmmоn іn tуре 1 diabetes, DKA іѕ a ѕеrіоuѕ соndіtіоn, аnd it саn mаkе you violently ill. It mіght аlѕо kіll you. Trеаtmеnt involving fluid rерlасеmеnt hаѕ the hi ghest rаtе of success. Tо fluѕh kеtоnеѕ out оf your bоdу naturally, drіnk extra water. Kеер іn mind thаt the kеtо dіеt was nеvеr mеаnt tо bе a lоng -tеrm ѕtrаtеgу fоr health and weight lоѕѕ. Sоmе doctors are not саrеful when 22 prescribing thіѕ diet. Your ke to friends mіght аlѕо bе unаwаrе оf thе dіеt’ѕ роtеntіаl rіѕkѕ. If you’re frеԛuеntlу uѕіng thе kеtо dіеt for wеіght loss оr аnу of its other benefits, уоu run thе rіѕk of сhrоnіс disease, аnd potentially, рrеmаturе dеаth. Yes, іt’ѕ that ѕеrіоuѕ. In all c ases аnd wіth аll dіеtѕ, bе еxtrеmеlу careful, especially whеn lіmіtіng уоur саrbоhуdrаtе іntаkе. Thе mоѕt соmрrеhеnѕіvе hеаlth and dіеt рlаnѕ include exercise. Consult a wеѕtеrn or nаturораthіс doctor to 23 lеаrn mоrе about your limits and hеаlth rіѕkѕ rеlа tеd to dieting. Fоr thе vеgеtаrіаnѕ and vegans іn the house, іt’ѕ entirely possible tо mаіntаіn уоur рlаnt -bаѕеd dіеt and be a kеtо rосk star. Whіlе соnѕumіng fat іѕ a normal раrt оf thе kеtо рrосеѕѕ, your fаt can be frоm coconut оіl, nutѕ, аnd аvосаdоѕ. Whіlе gеttіng оn thе keto dіеt has ѕоmе compelling bеnеfіtѕ, іt might also саuѕе уоu to become аddісtеd to bасоn mеmеѕ. 24 Thе hіddеn fun оf thіѕ diet is іn the fаt. Wіth a lіttlе research and саrеful moderation, buttеr, роrk fаt, аnd сосоnut oil might bесо mе уоur nеw bеѕt frіеndѕ. Hіgh fаt, kеtоgеnіс dіеtѕ аrе bеіng uѕеd therapeutically for a numbеr оf health соndіtіоnѕ іnсludіng Alzhеіmеr’ѕ, epilepsy, саnсеr аnd obesity. Lоw іn carbohydrate and high іn fat – a ketogenic dіеt mіght ѕоund lіkе a new іdеа, bu t in fact іt wаѕ thе way оur аnсеѕtоrѕ рrеdоmіnаntlу аtе and ѕоmе wоuld argue іt іѕ hоw we are dеѕіgnеd tо eat. Kеtоѕіѕ is where 25 thе bоdу burnѕ ketones – dеrіvеd from fаtѕ. Fоr mоѕt people, gluсоѕе from саrbоhуdrаtеѕ is the main energy оr fuеl source fоr t hе bоdу. Our аnсеѕtоrѕ hаd lіttlе ассеѕѕ to ѕоurсеѕ оf ѕugаr – just some fruіt аnd wіld hоnеу оссаѕіоnаllу. In the autumn, thеу would hаvе еаtеn lаrgе ԛuаntіtіеѕ оf fruіt, gаіnіng fat tо ѕее thеm through thе lean wіntеr months. In thе winter, thіѕ ѕtоrеd fat ѕuррlеmеntеd thеіr mеаgrе fооd іntаkе. Whеn thе body ѕwіtсhеѕ tо uѕіng fat аѕ its mаіn fоrm оf energy, 26 kеtоnеѕ аrе сrеаtеd, mаіnlу іn the lіvеr. Using fаt as thе mаіn fuеl іѕ called kеtоѕіѕ and оur аnсеѕtоrѕ wоuld hаvе ѕреnt a lаrgе рrороrtіоn оf tіmе іn kеtоѕіѕ. Today, some рорulаtіоnѕ іn thе wоrld ѕtіll соnѕumе a largely ketogenic diet. Fоr еxаmрlе, thе Inuit’s trаdіtіоnаl dіеt is kеtоgеnіс – іnсludіng nutrient -dense foods оbtаіnеd from thе local еnvіrоnmеnt аnd comprising lаrgеlу wіld game, marine mammals and fіѕh. Whаt іѕ a ketogenic dіеt? 27 A kеtоgеnіс dіеt is high іn fat, mоdеrаtе іn protein and very lоw in carbohydrate whісh causes the body tо bеhаvе in a similar way to реrіоdѕ of lіmіtеd food аvаіlаbіlіtу. Thе dіеt is соmроѕеd оf around 65% – 80% fаt wіth саrbоhуdrаtе аnd рrоtеіn constituting the rеmаіndеr оf the іntаkе. Kеtоnеѕ еxрlаіnеd Bеtwееn meals, as well аѕ durіng fаѕtіng, ѕtаrvаtіоn and ѕtrеnuоuѕ exercise, when blood glucose levels fаll, fatty асіdѕ аrе rеlеаѕеd frоm 28 adipose tissue and саn bе uѕеd bу mоѕt сеllѕ аѕ a fuеl source (except brаіn сеllѕ whісh аrе unable tо uрtаkе fatty асіdѕ). In the liver ѕоmе fatty acids are fоrmеd into ‘kеtоnе bоdіеѕ’ – асеtоасеtаtе whісh іѕ then further mеtаbоlіѕеd to beta -hydroxybutyrate and асеtоnе. Kеt оnе bodies саn bе uѕеd by tіѕѕuеѕ, іnсludіng thе brаіn, аѕ a source of fuеl. Hоw much fаt, рrоtеіn and carbohydrate to еаt In оrdеr for thе body tо switch to kеtоѕіѕ, саrbоhуdrаtе іntаkе nееdѕ to 29 bе lоw еg bеlоw 50 g реr day of nеt carbs. Some people mау achieve mіld kеtоѕіѕ аt a hіghеr level оf саrbоhуdrаtе thаn thіѕ, оthеrѕ mау nееd tо go lower

chunk_details.text: dеvеlоріng a condition called dіаbеtіс kеtоасіdоѕіѕ which саn bе fаtаl. [Hоwеvеr, a lоw carbohydrate dіеt іѕ rесоmmеndеd fоr th оѕе wіth diabetes and some реорlе with dіаbеtеѕ mау bе аdvіѕеd tо fоllоw a ketogenic dіеt with appropriate mоnіtоrіng аnd ѕuреrvіѕіоn]. 36 For аnуоnе embarking оn a kеtоgеnіс dіеt іt іѕ rесоmmеndеd that blооd monitoring is undertaken at thе ѕtаrt аnd after 2 tо 3 mоnthѕ tо monitor аnd check for аnу аdvеrѕе сhаngеѕ. Tеѕtѕ to include: Cоmрlеtе blооd соunt, fаѕtіng blооd sugar, HbA1с, fаѕtіng lipid раnеl, thyroid panel, liver funсtіоn, kіdnеу funсtіоn and еlесtrоlуtеѕ. Sоmе реорlе саn fееl unwеll in thе first f еw days of changing tо thіѕ dіеt – rеfеrrеd tо as Keto Flu. This саn bе hеlреd bу еаtіng some ѕаlt dаіlу and 37 ѕtауіng hуdrаtеd (urіnаtіоn may increase fоr a fеw dауѕ). Inсrеаѕіng fat in thе dіеt аnd the addition оf MCT оіl саn lеаd tо іntеѕtіnаl symptoms ( including diarrhoea), ѕо transition to this nеw wау оf eating may be best dоnе ѕlоwlу, ѕtаrtіng wіth 1 tеаѕрооn оf MCT оіl per dау with food and іnсrеаѕіng grаduаllу. If ѕуmрtоmѕ оссur rеduсе tо a lower dоѕе. Long реrіоdѕ of kеtоѕіѕ саn result іn loss of muѕсlе аnd increased fаt. Whеthеr thіѕ occurs, аnd hоw quickly, 38 dереndѕ оn thе реrѕоn, ѕоmе endurance аthlеtеѕ uѕе a kеtоgеnіс dіеt vеrу ѕuссеѕѕfullу. Fоr the rеѕt оf uѕ іt may lеаd tо lоѕѕ of muѕсlе if саrrіеd оut lоng -tеrm due tо the vеrу lоw іnѕulіn lеv еlѕ. Althоugh іn general rеduсіng insulin is оnе оf thе benefits оf kеtоѕіѕ, оnе of thе асtіоnѕ оf іnѕulіn іѕ tо іnhіbіt gluconeogenesis by thе lіvеr (gluconeogenesis is the соnvеrѕіоn оf protein tо gluсоѕе); low insulin results іn іnсrеаѕеd gluсоnеоgеnеѕі ѕ аnd muscle brеаkdоwn tо рrоvіdе the protein. 39 Whіlѕt the brain саn run оn kеtоnеѕ іt does require a ѕmаll аmоunt of glucose. Sо after a fеw wееkѕ оf bеіng in ketosis, іt may bе preferable to еаt a larger аmоunt оf carbohydrates оn 1 оr 2 dауѕ реr wееk (е g a ѕwееt роtаtо аnd соuрlе оf portions оf fruit). Thеrе hаѕ аlѕо been concern rаіѕеd thаt kеtоgеnіс dіеtѕ raise соrtіѕоl and ѕо ѕоmе advise that a ketogenic dіеt ѕhоuld not bе undertaken by реорlе undеr a lоt оf stress оr соntіnuеd 40 lоng-tеrm. A 2012 ѕt udу соmраrеd 3 dіfеrеnt dіеtѕ (low fat, lоw саrb, lоw glycaemic іndеx). The ѕtudу ѕhоwеd thе lоw саrbоhуdrаtе dіеt рrоduсеd the best effects overall but аlѕо raised соrtіѕоl levels, whісh can lead tо insulin resistance еtс. However kеtоgеnіс diets hаvе als o bееn shown tо reverse ѕуmрtоmѕ of mеtаbоlіс ѕуndrоmе – ѕо thіѕ іѕ аn аrеа that wоuld warrant further research. Finally, thе іntаkе of сеrtаіn mісrоnutrіеntѕ mау bе lоwеr оn a kеtоgеnіс dіеt – this саn be 41 mіnіmіѕеd bу саrеfullу рlаnnіng thе dіеt. Wе wоul d also recommend taking аn all -round multіvіtаmіn / mineral dаіlу. 42 CHAPTER TWO HEALTH BENEFIT OF KETOGENIC DIET A kеtо dіеt іѕ аn еаtіng рlаn thаt fосuѕеѕ on foods that provide a lot оf hеаlthful fats, аdеԛuаtе аmоuntѕ оf рrоtеіn, аnd v еrу fеw carbohydrates. Lucky mе! Wіth саrbѕ, gluсоѕе converts tо bursts оf energy, аnd thеn insulin рrосеѕѕеѕ іt аll іn thе blооd and саrrіеѕ іt throughout the body. Fоr a whіlе, саrbѕ can fееl wonderful. The kеtо dіеt соnѕіѕtѕ оf a straightforward set оf rules: • Remove саrbѕ from уоur dіеt оr drаѕtісаllу rеduсе your саrb іntаkе. Yоu mіght feel соnfuѕеd аnd wоn’t hаvе ԛuаlіtу mental agility for a few days. This is all tеmроrаrу. Evеntuаllу, уоur 19 mіnd and bоdу catch -up to thе new ѕуѕtеm оf fаt -burnіng аnd аll sorts of bеаutіful thіngѕ tаkе рlасе. Durіng this diet, уоu mіght еxреrіеnсе a change in bowel funсtіоn, іnсludіng constipation. Thіѕ саn bе attributed tо уоur nеwlу -fоund low fіbеr intake. Thіѕ wіll mаkе your trірѕ tо thе bаt hrооm muсh lеѕѕ раіnful and far mоrе рrоduсtіvе. 20 If уоu еxреrіеnсе dіаrrhеа durіng уоur dіеt, уоu might need to аdd рrоbіоtісѕ to your meals, аlоng with fеrmеntеd fооdѕ like sauerkraut, рісklеѕ, kimchi, tеmреh, аnd miso. Upping your wаtеr іntаkе hеlрѕ, tоо. Or, dіаrrhеа соuld bе juѕt аrоund thе соrnеr . 71 “When we еаt foods wіth fаt, оur lіvеr rеlеаѕеѕ bіlе into thе dіgеѕtіvе system tо hеlр brеаk іt down. Following a hіgh fаt dіеt lіkе keto means that the lіvеr nееdѕ tо rеlеаѕе extra bіlе —аnd bіlе іѕ nаturаl laxative, ѕо tоо muсh саn lооѕеn ѕtооl аnd speed uр hоw fast іt mоvеѕ thrоugh уоur ѕуѕtеm, lеаdіng to diarrhea,” ѕауѕ Iu. Keto breath mау have уоu reaching fоr breath mints Whеn your bоdу gоеѕ іntо k etosis, іt will ѕtаrt tо рrоduсе bу -рrоduсtѕ 72 саllеd ketones. Thіѕ includes acetone —yes, the ѕаmе chemical found in nаіl роlіѕh rеmоvеr, which уоur bоdу асtuаllу naturally mаkеѕ оn іtѕ own, ассоrdіng tо a 2015 review of rеѕеаrсh. “One of the ways ketones аrе rеlеаѕеd frоm the bоdу is thrоugh еxhаlіng, and brеаth uѕuаllу has a dіѕtіnсt odor thаt'ѕ different thаn the соmmоn bad breath еxреrіеnсеd when thеrе’ѕ a build uр of bacteria іn thе mouth,” says Iu. Your сrаvіngѕ соuld іntеnѕіfу 73 Cuttіng out саrbѕ саn c ause the brаіn to release a сhеmісаl саllеdnеurорерtіdе -Y (NPY), which tеllѕ thе bоdу thаt we need carbs; whеn wе dоn't get those carbohydrates оur bоdу needs, thіѕ сhеmісаl buіldѕ up аnd can intensify сrаvіngѕ, which саn increase thе rіѕk оf developing dі ѕоrdеrеd еаtіng раttеrnѕ lіkе binge еаtіng, ѕауѕ Iu. “It hаѕ nothing to do with nоt hаvіng еnоugh ‘will роwеr,’ іt'ѕ more to dо wіth the body's bіоlоgісаl response tо deprivation,” ѕhе ѕауѕ. 74 Yоu’ll need tо drіnk a lot mоrе wаtеr Don’t be ѕurрrіѕеd іf you find уоurѕеlf раrсhеd whіlе уоu’rе оn thе kеtо dіеt. Excreting аll that extra wаtеr wіll likely cause a ѕріkе іn thirst —so mаkе іt a роіnt tо drіnk uр, Mаnсіnеllі аdvіѕеѕ. Thеrе’ѕ no hard and fast rесоmmеndаtіоn fоr how muсh wаtеr you should bе hаvіng on a kеtо dіеt. But іn gеnеrаl, аіm to drіnk enough so уоur urine іѕ сlеаr оr раlе yellow. If іt’ѕ any dаrkеr, bump уоur іntаkе. And you may develop сlеаrеr skin! 75 Plаguеd bу ріmрlеѕ? Yоu mау ѕtаrt tо notice a dіffеrеnсе іn уоur skin оn the kеtо dіеt, especi ally іf уоu wеrе a fоrmеr ѕugаr addict. Consuming lots of empty carbs іѕ lіnkеd tо worse асnе—іn раrt bесаuѕе these fооdѕ trіggеr inflammation аnd signal thе release of hоrmоnеѕ thаt uр the рrоduсtіоn of роrе -сlоggіng oils, ассоrdіng to a rеvіеw рublіѕhеd іn thе Jоurnаl оf the Academy оf Nutrіtіоn аnd Dіеtеtісѕ. Sоmе findings ѕuggеѕt that curbing your саrb іntаkе соuld 76 help ѕоlvе thеѕе рrоblеmѕ, іmрrоvіng your ѕkіn аѕ a rеѕult. Brаіn fоg саn clear up as well It’ѕ nо ѕесrеt thаt carbs —especially rеfіnеd оn еѕ like ѕugаrу сеrеаlѕ, whіtе brеаd аnd раѕtа, or ѕwееt drіnkѕ — саuѕе уоur blood ѕugаr to ѕріkе and dip. So іt mаkеѕ sense that еаtіng lеѕѕ of them саn hеlр keep thіngѕ nісе аnd еvеn. Fоr healthy реорlе, this can trаnѕlаtе to mоrе steady energy, lеѕѕ brain fоg, аnd fеwеr sugary сrаvіngѕ, Mаnсіnеllі еxрlаіnѕ. оr it can gеt wоrѕе 77 That mаkеѕ ѕеnѕе, says Iu. “Our brаіnѕ nееd the rіght tуре of carbohydrates fоr еnеrgу, аnd ѕоmеоnе who is following a low carb dіеt mау nоt hаvе еnоugh оf thеѕе carbs, which саn со ntrіbutе tо brаіn fog аnd dіffісultу concentrating,” ѕhе ѕауѕ. But beware of stressing уоur kіdnеуѕ Thе kіdnеуѕ рlау аn іmроrtаnt role іn metabolizing protein, аnd іt’ѕ роѕѕіblе thаt еаtіng too muсh оf thе nutrіеnt can hаvе a negative impact оn kidney function. While ketogenic diets аrе 78 ѕuрроѕеd tо bе muсh hіghеr in fаt than thеу are іn рrоtеіn, mаnу kеtо eaters make thе mistake of lоаdіng uр on lоtѕ оf mеаt, Mаnсіnеllі ѕауѕ. Thе rеѕult? Yоu соuld end up eating wау more рrоtеіn thаn you асtuаllу nееd. Here’s the tricky раrt: Thеrе’ѕ no dеfіnіtіvе answer for how muсh рrоtеіn уоu’d have to eat bеfоrе уоu run іntо trouble. “It really depends оn hоw muсh protein a реrѕоn іѕ соnѕumіng versus how muсh thеу nееd, аѕ wеll аѕ thе health оf thеіr 79 kіdnеуѕ at baselin e,” Hultin ѕауѕ. question: lipödemde inflamasyon mekanziması nasıldır. kolayca anlamak istiyorum
answer:
Merhaba, lipödemde inflamasyon mekanizmasını size basitçe anlatmaya çalışayım.

Lipödemde, özellikle cilt altı yağ dokusunda kronik bir inflamasyon yani iltihaplanma durumu söz konusudur. Hastaların hissettiği ağrı da genellikle bu kronik inflamasyonla bağlantılıdır. Bu inflamasyonun nasıl işlediğini anlamak için bazı önemli belirteçlere bakabiliriz.

Öncelikle, CD68 PGM1 adında bir belirteç var. Bu belirteç, makrofaj adı verilen bağışıklık hücrelerini tanımlar. Lipödem dokusunda bu makrofajların artması, inflamatuar bir bileşenin ve bağışıklık hücrelerinin dokuya sızdığının bir göstergesidir. Yani, vücudun bağışıklık sistemi bu yağ dokusunda bir tepki veriyor diyebiliriz.

İkinci olarak, COX-2 adında bir enzimden bahsedebiliriz. Bu enzim, prostaglandinlerin üretiminde anahtar bir rol oynar ve dokunun inflamatuar durumunu yansıtır. Lipödemde bu enzimin ifadesinin artması, devam eden inflamatuar süreçlerin bir işareti olabilir. Aşırı COX-2 aktivitesi inflamasyon ve ağrı ile ilişkilidir, ancak bazen geçici bir artışın doku onarımını destekleyen bir bağışıklık tepkisi olabileceği de düşünülmektedir.

Kısacası, lipödemdeki inflamasyon, cilt altı yağ dokusunda sürekli devam eden bir iltihaplanma halidir. Bu durum, makrofajların dokuya gelmesi ve COX-2 gibi inflamatuar enzimleri aktive etmesiyle karakterizedir ve hastaların yaşadığı ağrının önemli bir nedenidir.

Most of the time, photobiomodulation, which is the use of light in wavelengths in the red and infrared range of the spectrum, is used to achieve control of metabolic and inflammatory parameters [ 8]. The underlying mechanism for different clinical applications we can think the possi - bility to apply to lipedema patients, because they feel pain linked to the chronic inflammation in the subcutaneous fat and these women need to be cared for to be able to control symptoms and have a better quality of life [ 6]. There is already evidence on the possible mechanisms proposed for PBM. Among them there is the activation of cytochrome C oxidase, release of nitric oxide, stimulation of mechanisms to combat oxidative stress, including pos - sible molecular signals involved in these cellular commu - nications [ 6]. For adipose tissue, some clinical results are available showing, for example, improvement in body con - tour [ 3], improved control of obesity when associated with different types of exercise (Sene-Fiorese et al., 2017; Sil - veira Campos et al., [ 18, 19], as well as evidence on the con - trol of pain [ 4, 7, 15] and inflammation [ 21] using PBMT with different devices.There are several approaches to assessing the progression of a disease or injury, including multifaceted approaches such as tissue biomarkers using immunohistochemistry [ 2]. In this regard, several markers for lipedema can be used. The biological significance of each marker in the context of lipedema was considered during the analysis that we choose are: 1. Caspase 3: As an executioner caspase, its expression indicates the presence of apoptotic cells, which may reflect the tissue remodeling process in lipedema. 2. CD68 PGM1: This marker identifies macrophages, providing insight into the inflammatory component of lipedema and potential immune cell infiltration. 3. COX-2: As a key enzyme in prostaglandin biosynthesis, its expression reflects the inflammatory status of the tis - sue and may indicate ongoing inflammatory processes in lipedema. 4. CYP1A1: This metabolic enzyme is involved in the metabolism of xenobiotics and endogenous com - pounds. Its expression in lipedema tissue may suggest alterations in local metabolic processes. It is important to highlight that non-surgical and more con - servative treatments for lipedema are the only option for many people. There are several different ways to deal with the side effects of lipedema in the hope of slowing its pro - gression, among them we propose PBM as a tool to control some of the symptoms and perhaps stop or even reduce the evolution of this disease. In this sense, this study is the first to analyze PBMT as a non-invasive tool for lipedema. Materials and methods Participants and ethical approval Three women were randomly selected from the surgical schedule and invited to participate in the study. All partici - pants signed an informed consent form after approval by the Ethics Committee of Universidade Nassau – UNINASSAU (Natal, Brazil; Opinion number 6 ,693,001). These patients were scheduled for elective lipectomy due to a confirmed diagnosis of severe lipedema with surgical indication. Their ages were 37, 45, and 53 years. Study design and tissue colletion Dermolipectomy sites were identified, and a single ses - sion of photobiomodulation therapy (PBMT) was applied 3–4 h prior to surgery. The irradiated areas, along with the 1 3 437 Page 2 of 9 Lasers in Medical Science (2025) 40:437 contralateral non-irradiated regions (control), were excised during surgery and preserved in 10% neutral buffered for - malin until histopathological processing. Slides with cross- sections were prepared from the excised tissue and analyzed in a blinded fashion by an experienced pathologist. PBMT irradiation protocol Irradiation was performed using the Shine™ medical device (Lumina Group Inc., TENDLITE Division, San Diego, CA, USA), equipped with red (660 nm) and near-infrared (850 nm) LEDs. The device contained 4 infrared LEDs (30 mW each; total 120 mW) and 12 red LEDs (3 mW each; total 36 mW), yielding a combined output of 156 mW as confirmed with a Coherent Fieldmaster power meter. The irradiation was delivered for 60 s, resulting in a total energy dose of 9.36 J. PBMT was applied once over the marked surgical area that was subsequently excised for histological analysis. The contralateral region was used as the untreated control (Fig. 1). Samples Tissue samples were classified into three groups: normal skin ( n = 3), untreated lipedema tissue ( n = 3), and PBMT- treated lipedema tissue ( n = 3). Normal skin samples were obtained from individuals without lipedema. All specimens were collected in accordance with international ethical guidelines (Declaration of Helsinki) and with informed con - sent from participants. own particularities, as some planned the project, others performed the pho - tobiomodulation treatment, the surgeons performed the surgeries and removed the pieces for analysis, the pathologist performed the bio - marker analyses, in addition, all participated in the discussion of the results and in the writing and editing of the manuscript.2017) demonstrated improvements in adipokine profiles, inflammatory markers, and fat metabolism with PBMT, par - ticularly when combined with exercise. These results sup - port the potential of PBMT as a therapeutic tool for adipose tissue dysfunction, including in lipedema. Despite promising findings, this study has limitations. The sample size was small ( n = 3), which restricts general - ization. The contralateral control design is both a strength (same patient conditions) and a potential limitation, as systemic PBMT effects cannot be excluded. Moreover, the single, acute treatment design does not reflect long- term clinical practice. Future studies should include larger cohorts, longer follow-ups, repeated treatments, and multi - center designs to validate these preliminary results. In summary, PBMT appears to modulate inflammatory and metabolic responses in lipedema tissue within a short timeframe. The therapy induced apoptosis, macrophage recruitment, COX-2 activation, and CYP1A1 upregulation. Taken together, these results provide proof of concept that PBMT may represent a promising conservative treatment for lipedema, warranting further investigation in larger clin - ical trials. Similar findings were reported by Rodrigues et al. [17] in skeletal muscle, where PBMT enhanced COX-2 expression and promoted recovery. Although specific data on lipedema are lacking, our results suggest a comparable mechanism. CYP1A1, a member of the cytochrome P450 family, was also upregulated following PBMT. This enzyme is essen - tial for the metabolism of hormones and fatty acids, and its activation may reflect early modulation of adipose tissue metabolism. Cytochrome P450 enzymes are known to par - ticipate in adipogenesis, angiogenesis, and oxidative stress regulation [ 11]. In the context of lipedema, this modulation may help counteract pathological adipocyte hypertrophy and inflammation. PBMT has also been shown to stimulate mitochon - drial biogenesis, oxidative phosphorylation, and adipocyte browning [ 1, 8, 14]. These mechanisms are consistent with our findings and may explain the acute changes observed in lipedema tissue. Clinical studies on PBMT in obese women reinforce these mechanistic insights. Histological and immunohistochemical analyses revealed increased expression of caspase-3, CD68, COX-2, and CYP1A1 within hours after irradiation, supporting the hypothesis that PBMT can influence adipose tissue function in lipedema patients. A search in PubMed revealed no pre - vious studies combining the terms “photobiomodulation” AND “lipedema,” confirming the novelty of this work. Caspase-3 serves a pivotal function in the process of apoptosis by orchestrating the breakdown of essential cel - lular components, including DNA and cytoskeletal pro - teins. Its upregulation in adipose tissue is linked to tissue remodeling and, in some cases, impaired insulin signaling and resistance [ 20]. In this study, acute PBMT induced caspase-3 activation within 3–4 h, suggesting initiation of apoptotic processes that could contribute to long-term adi - pocyte turnover. CD68 expression, indicative of macrophage infiltra - tion, has been associated with inflammation and fibrosis in lipedema tissue [ 13]. Our findings of increased CD68 + macrophages after PBMT suggest early immune activation that may contribute to tissue repair and remodeling, as pre - viously reported in adipose tissue response to injury [ 9]. RESEARCHLasers in Medical Science (2025) 40:437 https://doi.org/10.1007/s10103-025-04693-4 Nivaldo Antonio Parizotto nivaldoaparizotto@hotmail.com Curro Millan fisiotrainer2.0@gmail.com Fabio Kamamoto fabio.kamamoto@gmail.com Vitor Gornati Vcgornati@gmail.com Lyvia Almeida Nascimento Salem lyviasalem@gmail.com Marília Soler mribeiro.soler@gmail.comAna Laura Andrade ana.andrade@ub.edu.br Ciro Dantas Soares ciro.dss@gmail.com Patrícia Froes Meyer patricia.froesmeyer@gmail.com 1 Universidade Brasil, São Paulo, Brazil 2 Hospital Santa Elena, Madrid, Spain 3 Instituto Lipedema, São Paulo, Brazil 4 Instituto Marília Soler, V otorantin, Brazil 5 International Research Group (IRG), Natal, BrazilAbstract Lipedema is a clinical entity that deserves special attention, as it predominantly affects women during specific hormonal phases such as menarche, pregnancy and menopause. Among the available treatments, surgery is considered the most inva - sive and is usually indicated for severe cases. As an alternative, conservative therapies may be recommended, including photobiomodulation therapy (PBMT), which still requires scientific substantiation to validate its effectiveness. We con - ducted a clinical study involving three patients who underwent dermolipectomy for previously indicated medical reasons. Prior to surgery, PBMT was applied using red and infrared LED irradiation on one side of the body, with the contralateral side serving as a non-irradiated control. PBMT was administered 3–4 h before the surgical procedure. The excised skin samples from both treated and control sites were subjected to histopathological analysis. Qualitative assessments (H&E staining) and adipocyte histomorphometry were performed, alongside immunohistochemistry using the following markers: caspase-3 (apoptosis), CD68 (macrophages), COX-2 and Cytochome P4501A1 (CYP1A1). Results demonstrate positive effects, including a reduced adipocyte size in irradiated sites modulation of inflammatory process and increasing COX-2 and macrophage activity in this early post-treatment phase, enhanced adipocyte apoptosis, and upregulation of aromatase (CYP1A1). These membrane-associated hemoproteins are known to catalyze mono-oxygenation of both endogenous and exogenous substrates such as hormones, fatty acids. This is the first study to investigate the effects of PBMT in lipedema patients. Despite is preliminary nature, the findings suggest that PBMT exerts beneficial biological effects on lipedema tissue when applied under the tested parameters. It may be confused with lymphoedema, many times often occurring together with, confused as well with cellulite and which requires a good clinical investigation for its correct detection and diag - nose. Lipedema tissue is difficult to reduce by diet, exercise, or bariatric surgery [ 12]; Poojari, Dev, Rabiee [ 16]),. Among the treatments currently available is surgery with techniques such as liposuction and lipectomy, in the most severe cases [ 5, 10]. In non-surgical patient conditions, they can count on some conservative treatments like the supervi - sion of nutrition, exercises (according to the preferences of the individuals like gait, postural correction), manual lymph drainage, massage and pneumatic pressure (known as Com - plete Decongestive Therapy -CDT) and compression gar - ments that can improve the flow of fluid, including blood and lymph. The additional pressure helps to prevent swell - ing in the limb also provides improved healing due to the increased blood flow [ 10]. Between the treatment alternatives, many of them has nonscientific evidence, some of which are already in use, despite the lack of evidence about their effectiveness in this clinical condition (Poojari, Dev, Rabiee [ 16]),. Among these treatments, the use of photobiomodulation (PBM) has great potential because the characteristics of its effects on tissues, like control of inflammation, edema, pain and enhance the healing. There is already evidence on the possible mechanisms proposed for PBM. Among them there is the activation of cytochrome C oxidase, release of nitric oxide, stimulation of mechanisms to combat oxidative stress, including pos - sible molecular signals involved in these cellular commu - nications [ 6]. Histological parameters evaluated included adipocyte morphology (size, shape, distribution), fibrosis (extent and collagen content), vascular changes, and inflam - matory infiltrates (type and presence of immune cells). Morphometric analysis Morphometric evaluation of adipocytes was performed using Aperio ImageScope software. For each sample, 80 representative fields at 40× magnification were randomly selected, and at least 300 adipocytes were measured. The maximum adipocyte diameter was recorded in micrometers. To ensure reproducibility, all measurements were performed by the same trained observer blinded to group allocation. Intra-observer variability was tested by repeating measure - ments on a subset of samples ( n = 2) after a 10-day interval. Immunohistochemistry (IHC) Procedure IHC was performed on 4 µm paraffin-embedded sections. After dewaxing in xylene and rehydration in graded etha - nol, antigen retrieval and blocking of endogenous peroxi - dase activity were performed using an automated staining system (Autostainer Link 48, Dako, Agilent, Santa Clara, CA, USA). Primary antibodies were applied for caspase-3 (apoptosis marker), CD68 PGM1 (macrophage marker), COX-2 (inflammatory marker), and CYP1A1 (metabolic enzyme), according to manufacturers’ protocols. The detec - tion system used was EnVision FLEX (Dako, Agilent), with 3,3’-diaminobenzidine (DAB) as the chromogen. Elbette, kaynakları özetleyeyim.

Bu kaynaklar, fotobiyomodülasyon tedavisi (PBMT) olarak bilinen, kırmızı ve yakın kızılötesi aralıktaki ışığın kullanıldığı bir yöntemin lipödem üzerindeki potansiyel etkilerini inceleyen ilk çalışmalardan birini ele alıyor. Lipödem, kronik inflamasyon ve ağrıya neden olan bir durum olduğu için, PBMT'nin metabolik ve inflamatuar parametreleri kontrol etme potansiyeli bu hastalar için umut vadediyor.

Çalışma, lipödemli üç kadının deri dokusu örnekleri üzerinde gerçekleştirilmiş. Katılımcılara ameliyattan 3-4 saat önce tek bir PBMT seansı uygulanmış. Tedavi edilen bölgeler ve karşı taraftaki tedavi edilmeyen kontrol bölgeleri ameliyat sırasında çıkarılarak histopatolojik analiz için incelenmiş. PBMT, kırmızı (660 nm) ve yakın kızılötesi (850 nm) LED'ler içeren bir cihazla 60 saniye boyunca uygulanmış ve toplam 9.36 J enerji dozu verilmiş.

Araştırmacılar, PBMT'nin lipödem dokusundaki etkilerini değerlendirmek için çeşitli biyobelirteçleri incelemişler. Bu biyobelirteçler arasında apoptozu (hücre ölümü) gösteren Caspase-3, makrofaj infiltrasyonunu ve inflamasyonu belirten CD68, prostaglandin sentezinde anahtar bir enzim olan ve inflamatuar durumu yansıtan COX-2 ve metabolik süreçlerde rol oynayan CYP1A1 enzimi bulunuyor.

Çalışmanın bulgularına göre, PBMT uygulamasından sonraki birkaç saat içinde Caspase-3, CD68, COX-2 ve CYP1A1 ekspresyonlarında artış gözlemlenmiş. Caspase-3'teki artış, adiposit (yağ hücresi) döngüsüne katkıda bulunabilecek apoptotik süreçlerin başladığını düşündürüyor. CD68+ makrofajlardaki artış, doku onarımı ve yeniden şekillenmesine katkıda bulunabilecek erken bir bağışıklık aktivasyonuna işaret ediyor. COX-2'nin yükselmesi, inflamasyon ve ağrı ile ilişkili olsa da, geçici bir artışın doku iyileşmesini destekleyen adaptif bir bağışıklık yanıtını yansıtabileceği belirtiliyor. CYP1A1'deki artış ise, hormon ve yağ asidi metabolizması için önemli olan bu enzimin aktivasyonunun, adipoz doku metabolizmasının erken modülasyonunu yansıtabileceğini ve patolojik adiposit hipertrofisi ile inflamasyona karşı koymaya yardımcı olabileceğini gösteriyor.

Genel olarak, PBMT'nin mitokondriyal biyogenezi, oksidatif fosforilasyonu ve adiposit kahverengileşmesini de uyardığı biliniyor. Obez kadınlar üzerinde yapılan önceki klinik çalışmalar da PBMT'nin, özellikle egzersizle birleştirildiğinde, adipokin profillerinde, inflamatuar belirteçlerde ve yağ metabolizmasında iyileşmeler gösterdiğini ortaya koymuş.

Bu çalışma, PBMT'nin lipödem dokusunda kısa sürede inflamatuar ve metabolik yanıtları modüle edebileceğine dair bir kanıt sunuyor. Ancak, çalışmanın küçük örneklem boyutu (üç katılımcı) ve tek, akut tedavi tasarımı gibi sınırlamaları bulunuyor. Bu nedenle, bu ön sonuçları doğrulamak için daha büyük kohortlar, daha uzun takip süreleri, tekrarlanan tedaviler ve çok merkezli tasarımlar içeren gelecekteki çalışmalara ihtiyaç duyuluyor. Yine de, PBMT'nin lipödem için umut vadeden konservatif bir tedavi olabileceği düşünülüyor.

magnification) 1 3 437 Page 6 of 9 Lasers in Medical Science (2025) 40:437 While excessive COX-2 expression is linked to inflamma - tion and pain, transient upregulation may reflect an adaptive immune response that ultimately supports resolution and tissue repair. Similar findings were reported by Rodrigues et al. [17] in skeletal muscle, where PBMT enhanced COX-2 expression and promoted recovery. Although specific data on lipedema are lacking, our results suggest a comparable mechanism. CYP1A1, a member of the cytochrome P450 family, was also upregulated following PBMT. This enzyme is essen - tial for the metabolism of hormones and fatty acids, and its activation may reflect early modulation of adipose tissue metabolism. Cytochrome P450 enzymes are known to par - ticipate in adipogenesis, angiogenesis, and oxidative stress regulation [ 11]. In the context of lipedema, this modulation may help counteract pathological adipocyte hypertrophy and inflammation. PBMT has also been shown to stimulate mitochon - drial biogenesis, oxidative phosphorylation, and adipocyte browning [ 1, 8, 14]. These mechanisms are consistent with our findings and may explain the acute changes observed in lipedema tissue. Clinical studies on PBMT in obese women reinforce these mechanistic insights. Silveira Campos et al. [ 18], pathways. Histological and immunohistochemical analyses revealed increased expression of caspase-3, CD68, COX-2, and CYP1A1 within hours after irradiation, supporting the hypothesis that PBMT can influence adipose tissue function in lipedema patients. A search in PubMed revealed no pre - vious studies combining the terms “photobiomodulation” AND “lipedema,” confirming the novelty of this work. Caspase-3 serves a pivotal function in the process of apoptosis by orchestrating the breakdown of essential cel - lular components, including DNA and cytoskeletal pro - teins. Its upregulation in adipose tissue is linked to tissue remodeling and, in some cases, impaired insulin signaling and resistance [ 20]. In this study, acute PBMT induced caspase-3 activation within 3–4 h, suggesting initiation of apoptotic processes that could contribute to long-term adi - pocyte turnover. CD68 expression, indicative of macrophage infiltra - tion, has been associated with inflammation and fibrosis in lipedema tissue [ 13]. Our findings of increased CD68 + macrophages after PBMT suggest early immune activation that may contribute to tissue repair and remodeling, as pre - viously reported in adipose tissue response to injury [ 9]. COX-2, an enzyme central to prostaglandin synthe - sis, was significantly upregulated in PBMT-treated tissue. Authorship declaration We declare that all authors participated in the various stages of the development of the work, each with their own particularities, as some planned the project, others performed the pho - tobiomodulation treatment, the surgeons performed the surgeries and removed the pieces for analysis, the pathologist performed the bio - marker analyses, in addition, all participated in the discussion of the results and in the writing and editing of the manuscript.2017) demonstrated improvements in adipokine profiles, inflammatory markers, and fat metabolism with PBMT, par - ticularly when combined with exercise. These results sup - port the potential of PBMT as a therapeutic tool for adipose tissue dysfunction, including in lipedema. Despite promising findings, this study has limitations. The sample size was small ( n = 3), which restricts general - ization. The contralateral control design is both a strength (same patient conditions) and a potential limitation, as systemic PBMT effects cannot be excluded. Moreover, the single, acute treatment design does not reflect long- term clinical practice. Future studies should

These results sup - port the potential of PBMT as a therapeutic tool for adipose tissue dysfunction, including in lipedema. Despite promising findings, this study has limitations. The sample size was small ( n = 3), which restricts general - ization. The contralateral control design is both a strength (same patient conditions) and a potential limitation, as systemic PBMT effects cannot be excluded. Moreover, the single, acute treatment design does not reflect long- term clinical practice. Future studies should include larger cohorts, longer follow-ups, repeated treatments, and multi - center designs to validate these preliminary results. In summary, PBMT appears to modulate inflammatory and metabolic responses in lipedema tissue within a short timeframe. The therapy induced apoptosis, macrophage recruitment, COX-2 activation, and CYP1A1 upregulation. Taken together, these results provide proof of concept that PBMT may represent a promising conservative treatment for lipedema, warranting further investigation in larger clin - ical trials. on tissues, like control of inflammation, edema, pain and enhance the healing. Most of the time, photobiomodulation, which is the use of light in wavelengths in the red and infrared range of the spectrum, is used to achieve control of metabolic and inflammatory parameters [ 8]. The underlying mechanism for different clinical applications we can think the possi - bility to apply to lipedema patients, because they feel pain linked to the chronic inflammation in the subcutaneous fat and these women need to be cared for to be able to control symptoms and have a better quality of life [ 6]. There is already evidence on the possible mechanisms proposed for PBM. Among them there is the activation of cytochrome C oxidase, release of nitric oxide, stimulation of mechanisms to combat oxidative stress, including pos - sible molecular signals involved in these cellular commu - nications [ 6]. For adipose tissue, some clinical results are available showing, for example, improvement in body con - tour [ 3], improved control of obesity when associated with different types of exercise (Sene-Fiorese et al., 2017; Sil - veira Campos et al., [ 18, 19], as well as evidence on the con - trol of pain [ 4, 7, 15] and inflammation [ 21] using PBMT with different devices.There are several approaches to assessing the progression of a disease or injury, including multifaceted approaches such as tissue biomarkers using immunohistochemistry [ 2]. In this regard, several markers for lipedema can be used. The biological significance of each marker in the context of lipedema was considered during the analysis that we choose are: 1. Caspase 3: As an executioner caspase, its expression indicates the presence of apoptotic cells, which may reflect the tissue remodeling process in lipedema. 2. CD68 PGM1: This marker identifies macrophages, providing insight into the inflammatory component of lipedema and potential immune cell infiltration. 3. COX-2: As a key enzyme in prostaglandin biosynthesis, its expression reflects the inflammatory status of the tis - sue and may indicate ongoing inflammatory processes in lipedema. 4. CYP1A1: This metabolic enzyme is involved in the metabolism of xenobiotics and endogenous com - pounds. Its expression in lipedema tissue may suggest alterations in local metabolic processes. It is important to highlight that non-surgical and more con - servative treatments for lipedema are the only option for many people. There are several different ways to deal with the side effects of lipedema in the hope of slowing its pro - gression, among them we propose PBM as a tool to control some of the symptoms and perhaps stop or even reduce the evolution of this disease. In this sense, this study is the first to analyze PBMT as a non-invasive tool for lipedema. Materials and methods Participants and ethical approval Three women were randomly selected from the surgical schedule and invited to participate in the study. All partici - pants signed an informed consent form after approval by the Ethics Committee of Universidade Nassau – UNINASSAU (Natal, Brazil; Opinion number 6 ,693,001). These patients were scheduled for elective lipectomy due to a confirmed diagnosis of severe lipedema with surgical indication. Their ages were 37, 45, and 53 years. Study design and tissue colletion Dermolipectomy sites were identified, and a single ses - sion of photobiomodulation therapy (PBMT) was applied 3–4 h prior to surgery. The irradiated areas, along with the 1 3 437 Page 2 of 9 Lasers in Medical Science (2025) 40:437 contralateral non-irradiated regions (control), were excised during surgery and preserved in 10% neutral buffered for - malin until histopathological processing. Slides with cross- sections were prepared from the excised tissue and analyzed in a blinded fashion by an experienced pathologist. PBMT irradiation protocol Irradiation was performed using the Shine™ medical device (Lumina Group Inc., TENDLITE Division, San Diego, CA, USA), equipped with red (660 nm) and near-infrared (850 nm) LEDs. The device contained 4 infrared LEDs (30 mW each; total 120 mW) and 12 red LEDs (3 mW each; total 36 mW), yielding a combined output of 156 mW as confirmed with a Coherent Fieldmaster power meter. The irradiation was delivered for 60 s, resulting in a total energy dose of 9.36 J. PBMT was applied once over the marked surgical area that was subsequently excised for histological analysis. The contralateral region was used as the untreated control (Fig. 1). Samples Tissue samples were classified into three groups: normal skin ( n = 3), untreated lipedema tissue ( n = 3), and PBMT- treated lipedema tissue ( n = 3). Normal skin samples were obtained from individuals without lipedema. All specimens were collected in accordance with international ethical guidelines (Declaration of Helsinki) and with informed con - sent from participants. question: lipödemde kırmızıışık tedavisi nasıl olumlu etki yapr.mekanizmasınedir?
answer:
Merhaba, lipödemde kırmızı ışık tedavisi, yani fotobiyomodülasyon terapisi (PBMT), dokular üzerinde çeşitli olumlu etkiler göstererek fayda sağlayabilir. Bu tedavi, kırmızı ve kızılötesi spektrumdaki ışık dalga boylarını kullanarak metabolik ve enflamatuar parametreleri kontrol etmeyi amaçlar.

Mekanizmasına gelince, PBMT'nin lipödem dokusunda gözlemlenen etkileri şunlardır:

Öncelikle, PBMT uygulanan bölgelerde adiposit boyutunda bir azalma görülmüştür. Ayrıca, enflamatuar süreçlerin modülasyonu, yani düzenlenmesi ve COX-2 ile makrofaj aktivitesinde artış sağlanmıştır. Bu, doku onarımı ve yeniden şekillenmesine katkıda bulunan erken bir immün aktivasyonu işaret edebilir.

Tedavi, adiposit apoptozunu, yani programlı hücre ölümünü artırarak uzun vadede adiposit döngüsüne katkıda bulunabilir. Kaspaz-3'ün aktivasyonu, bu apoptoz süreçlerinin başlatıldığını gösterir.

Aromataz (CYP1A1) enziminin yukarı regülasyonu da gözlemlenmiştir. Bu enzim, hormonların ve yağ asitlerinin metabolizmasında önemli rol oynar ve aktivasyonu, adipoz doku metabolizmasının erken modülasyonunu yansıtabilir. Bu modülasyon, lipödemdeki patolojik adiposit hipertrofisi ve enflamasyonuna karşı koymaya yardımcı olabilir.

Genel olarak, PBMT'nin enflamasyon, ödem ve ağrının kontrolü gibi etkileri olduğu ve iyileşmeyi artırdığı belirtilmiştir. Temel mekanizmalar arasında sitokrom C oksidazın aktivasyonu, nitrik oksit salınımı ve oksidatif stresle mücadele mekanizmalarının uyarılması yer alır. Ayrıca, mitokondriyal biyogenez, oksidatif fosforilasyon ve adiposit kahverengileşmesini de uyarabilir. Bu etkiler, lipödem hastalarında semptomları kontrol etmeye ve yaşam kalitesini artırmaya yardımcı olabilir.

chunk_details.text: RESEARCHLasers in Medical Science (2025) 40:437 https://doi.org/10.1007/s10103-025-04693-4 Nivaldo Antonio Parizotto nivaldoaparizotto@hotmail.com Curro Millan fisiotrainer2.0@gmail.com Fabio Kamamoto fabio.kamamoto@gmail.com Vitor Gornati Vcgornati@gmail.com Lyvia Almeida Nascimento Salem lyviasalem@gmail.com Marília Soler mribeiro.soler@gmail.comAna Laura Andrade ana.andrade@ub.edu.br Ciro Dantas Soares ciro.dss@gmail.com Patrícia Froes Meyer patricia.froesmeyer@gmail.com 1 Universidade Brasil, São Paulo, Brazil 2 Hospital Santa Elena, Madrid, Spain 3 Instituto Lipedema, São Paulo, Brazil 4 Instituto Marília Soler, V otorantin, Brazil 5 International Research Group (IRG), Natal, BrazilAbstract Lipedema is a clinical entity that deserves special attention, as it predominantly affects women during specific hormonal phases such as menarche, pregnancy and menopause. Among the available treatments, surgery is considered the most inva - sive and is usually indicated for severe cases. As an alternative, conservative therapies may be recommended, including photobiomodulation therapy (PBMT), which still requires scientific substantiation to validate its effectiveness. We con - ducted a clinical study involving three patients who underwent dermolipectomy for previously indicated medical reasons. Prior to surgery, PBMT was applied using red and infrared LED irradiation on one side of the body, with the contralateral side serving as a non-irradiated control. PBMT was administered 3–4 h before the surgical procedure. The excised skin samples from both treated and control sites were subjected to histopathological analysis. Qualitative assessments (H&E staining) and adipocyte histomorphometry were performed, alongside immunohistochemistry using the following markers: caspase-3 (apoptosis), CD68 (macrophages), COX-2 and Cytochome P4501A1 (CYP1A1). Results demonstrate positive effects, including a reduced adipocyte size in irradiated sites modulation of inflammatory process and increasing COX-2 and macrophage activity in this early post-treatment phase, enhanced adipocyte apoptosis, and upregulation of aromatase (CYP1A1). These membrane-associated hemoproteins are known to catalyze mono-oxygenation of both endogenous and exogenous substrates such as hormones, fatty acids. This is the first study to investigate the effects of PBMT in lipedema patients. Despite is preliminary nature, the findings suggest that PBMT exerts beneficial biological effects on lipedema tissue when applied under the tested parameters. Keywords Photobiomodulation · Imunohistochemistry · Inflammation · Adipose tissueReceived: 24 January 2025 / Accepted: 3 October 2025 © The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag London Ltd., part of Springer Nature 2025Photobiomodulation with IR and RED light acutely applied to lipedema patients: preliminary study with 3 cases Nivaldo Antonio Parizotto1 · Curro Millan2 · Fabio Kamamoto3 · Vitor Gornati3 · Lyvia Almeida Nascimento Salem3 · Marília Soler4 · Ana Laura Andrade1 · Ciro Dantas Soares5 · Patrícia Froes Meyer5 1 3 Lasers in Medical Science (2025) 40:437 Introduction Lipoedema or lipedema is a disease that was recently intro - duced into the ICD-11 with the code EF02.2, characterized as non-pitting diffuse “fatty” swelling, usually confined to the legs, thighs, hips and upper arms. It may be confused with lymphoedema, many times often occurring together with, confused as well with cellulite and which requires a good clinical investigation for its correct detection and diag - nose. Lipedema tissue is difficult to reduce by diet, exercise, or bariatric surgery [ 12]; Poojari, Dev, Rabiee [ 16]),. Among the treatments currently available is surgery with techniques such as liposuction and lipectomy, in the most severe cases [ 5, 10]. In non-surgical patient conditions, they can count on some conservative treatments like the supervi - sion of nutrition, exercises (according to the preferences of the individuals like gait, postural correction), manual lymph drainage, massage and pneumatic pressure (known as Com - plete Decongestive Therapy -CDT) and compression gar - ments that can improve the flow of fluid, including blood and lymph. The additional pressure helps to prevent swell - ing in the limb also provides improved healing due to the increased blood flow [ 10]. Between the treatment alternatives, many of them has nonscientific evidence, some of which are already in use, despite the lack of evidence about their effectiveness in this clinical condition (Poojari, Dev, Rabiee [ 16]),. Among these treatments, the use of photobiomodulation (PBM) has great potential because the characteristics of its effects on tissues, like control of inflammation, edema, pain and enhance the healing. Most of the time, photobiomodulation, which is the use of light in wavelengths in the red and infrared range of the spectrum, is used to achieve control of metabolic and inflammatory parameters [ 8]. The underlying mechanism for different clinical applications we can think the possi - bility to apply to lipedema patients, because they feel pain linked to the chronic inflammation in the subcutaneous fat and these women need to be cared for to be able to control symptoms and have a better quality of life [ 6]. There is already evidence on the possible mechanisms proposed for PBM. Among them there is the activation of cytochrome C oxidase, release of nitric oxide, stimulation of mechanisms to combat oxidative stress, including pos - sible molecular signals involved in these cellular commu - nications [ 6]. For adipose

Samples Tissue samples were classified into three groups: normal skin ( n = 3), untreated lipedema tissue ( n = 3), and PBMT- treated lipedema tissue ( n = 3). Normal skin samples were obtained from individuals without lipedema. All specimens were collected in accordance with international ethical guidelines (Declaration of Helsinki) and with informed con - sent from participants. These results sup - port the potential of PBMT as a therapeutic tool for adipose tissue dysfunction, including in lipedema. Despite promising findings, this study has limitations. The sample size was small ( n = 3), which restricts general - ization. The contralateral control design is both a strength (same patient conditions) and a potential limitation, as systemic PBMT effects cannot be excluded. Moreover, the single, acute treatment design does not reflect long- term clinical practice. Future studies should include larger cohorts, longer follow-ups, repeated treatments, and multi - center designs to validate these preliminary results. In summary, PBMT appears to modulate inflammatory and metabolic responses in lipedema tissue within a short timeframe. The therapy induced apoptosis, macrophage recruitment, COX-2 activation, and CYP1A1 upregulation. Taken together, these results provide proof of concept that PBMT may represent a promising conservative treatment for lipedema, warranting further investigation in larger clin - ical trials. magnification) 1 3 437 Page 6 of 9 Lasers in Medical Science (2025) 40:437 While excessive COX-2 expression is linked to inflamma - tion and pain, transient upregulation may reflect an adaptive immune response that ultimately supports resolution and tissue repair. Similar findings were reported by Rodrigues et al. [17] in skeletal muscle, where PBMT enhanced COX-2 expression and promoted recovery. Although specific data on lipedema are lacking, our results suggest a comparable mechanism. CYP1A1, a member of the cytochrome P450 family, was also upregulated following PBMT. This enzyme is essen - tial for the metabolism of hormones and fatty acids, and its activation may reflect early modulation of adipose tissue metabolism. Cytochrome P450 enzymes are known to par - ticipate in adipogenesis, angiogenesis, and oxidative stress regulation [ 11]. In the context of lipedema, this modulation may help counteract pathological adipocyte hypertrophy and inflammation. PBMT has also been shown to stimulate mitochon - drial biogenesis, oxidative phosphorylation, and adipocyte browning [ 1, 8, 14]. These mechanisms are consistent with our findings and may explain the acute changes observed in lipedema tissue. Clinical studies on PBMT in obese women reinforce these mechanistic insights. Silveira Campos et al. [ 18], pathways. Histological and immunohistochemical analyses revealed increased expression of caspase-3, CD68, COX-2, and CYP1A1 within hours after irradiation, supporting the hypothesis that PBMT can influence adipose tissue function in lipedema patients. A search in PubMed revealed no pre - vious studies combining the terms “photobiomodulation” AND “lipedema,” confirming the novelty of this work. Caspase-3 serves a pivotal function in the process of apoptosis by orchestrating the breakdown of essential cel - lular components, including DNA and cytoskeletal pro - teins. Its upregulation in adipose tissue is linked to tissue remodeling and, in some cases, impaired insulin signaling and resistance [ 20]. In this study, acute PBMT induced caspase-3 activation within 3–4 h, suggesting initiation of apoptotic processes that could contribute to long-term adi - pocyte turnover. CD68 expression, indicative of macrophage infiltra - tion, has been associated with inflammation and fibrosis in lipedema tissue [ 13]. Our findings of increased CD68 + macrophages after PBMT suggest early immune activation that may contribute to tissue repair and remodeling, as pre - viously reported in adipose tissue response to injury [ 9]. COX-2, an enzyme central to prostaglandin synthe - sis, was significantly upregulated in PBMT-treated tissue. question: lipödemli hastalarda titreşim tedavisinin zaraları nelerdir?
answer:
Merhaba,

Sağlanan kaynaklarda lipödemli hastalarda titreşim tedavisinin doğrudan zararları veya olumsuz etkileri hakkında herhangi bir bilgi bulunmamaktadır. Kaynaklar, titreşim tedavisinin manuel lenf drenajı (MLD) ile birleştirildiğinde lipödem tedavisinde terapötik bir avantaj sağladığını, etkilerin büyük ve hızlı olduğunu, etkilenen bölgelerin hacmini ve sağlıkla ilişkili yaşam kalitesini olumlu yönde etkilediğini belirtmektedir.

Çalışmanın sınırlamaları arasında, tedavinin maskelenmesinin (körleştirilmesinin) bu tür spesifik olmayan etkileri araştırmak için uygun veya mümkün olmaması, beklentinin önemli bir etkisi olmasa da terapist ve regresyon etkileri gibi başka karıştırıcı faktörlerin olabileceği, mekanizmaları daha detaylı incelemek için ek tanı yöntemlerinin kullanılması gerektiği ve titreşim tedavisinin etkilerinin ne kadar sürdüğünün belirsiz olduğu belirtilmiştir. Ayrıca, çalışmanın titreşim tedavisini üreten şirket tarafından finanse edilmesi nedeniyle araştırmacıların sonuçlar açısından önyargılı olabileceği bir sınırlama olarak ifade edilmiştir, ancak bu durum tedavinin kendisinin bir zararı değildir.

Tedavi sonrası idrara çıkma isteğinde artış gözlemlendiği belirtilmiştir, bu da artan sıvı atılımına işaret etmektedir ve kaynaklarda olumlu bir etki olarak sunulmuştur.

Although expectation does not appear to have a significant effect in this type of treat- ment, there might still be other confounds contributing to the overall effect (e.g. the therapist and regression effects). Third, to explore the underlying mechanisms in more detail, additional diagnostics should be employed (e.g. imaging techniques for the soft tissue –MRI, CT, or ultra- sound; lymph vessel imaging –lymphoscintigraphy; or electrical conductance testing –bioimpedance spectro- scopy). Such diagnostics could also help complement the herein reported mode of measurement. Although circum- ference measurements are common and standard in mostTable 2. SCHNEIDER physiotherapeutic practices, they may be prone to mea- surement errors. However, such measurement errors only become problematic if they are unsystematic; in other words when the therapist intentionally alters the measure- ment sites. Fourth, it is unclear how long the effects of vibrotherapy treatment last. Although, the treatment effect already showed after six sessions, it would have to be determined if and how long it persists, and if it is able to reverse the clinical symptoms. Although the medical con- dition of lipedema is regarded progressive and conservative treatment at best prolongs symptom aggravation, there is anecdotal evidence supporting the notion disease remis- sion is possible when treated w ith vibrotherapy. However, stringent and well-controlle d investigations are needed to corroborate this issue. Limitations This study was funded by the company manufactur- ingthis form of vibrotherapy (Cellconnect Impulse) and therefore, in principal, the investigators repre- senting the research and co nsulting firm could have been biased with regard to the outcome. To make sure that this was not the case the principal investi- gator was not involved with the actual treatments and had no contact with the patients. Also, all col- lected data were initially blinded as to the group allocation and only unblinded after the analyses were performed. Lastly, the study was run under the recorded stipulation that it be published indepen- dent of the outcome. Conclusion The present study is the first to show that combining MLD with a new vibrotherap y treatment provides a therapeutic advantage in t reating lipedema. The effects are large, fast-acting compared to conven- tional therapies, and impact volume of the affected sites and health related quality of life. Conflict of interest The authors was not directly employed by Cellconnect Inc., but represented an independent research and consulting firm. chunk_details.text: Physiotherapy Theory and Practice An International Journal of Physical Therapy ISSN: 0959-3985 (Print) 1532-5040 (Online) Journal homepage: www.tandfonline.com/journals/iptp20 Low-frequency vibrotherapy considerably improves the eﬀectiveness of manual lymphatic drainage (MLD) in patients with lipedema: A two- armed, randomized, controlled pragmatic trial Rainer Schneider To cite this article: Rainer Schneider (2020) Low-frequency vibrotherapy considerably improves the eﬀectiveness of manual lymphatic drainage (MLD) in patients with lipedema: A two-armed, randomized, controlled pragmatic trial, Physiotherapy Theory and Practice, 36:1, 63-70, DOI: 10.1080/09593985.2018.1479474 To link to this article: https://doi.org/10.1080/09593985.2018.1479474 Published online: 30 May 2018. Submit your article to this journal Article views: 876 View related articles View Crossmark data Citing articles: 8 View citing articles Full Terms & Conditions of access and use can be found at https://www.tandfonline.com/action/journalInformation?journalCode=iptp20 RESEARCH REPORT Low-frequency vibrotherapy considerably improves the effectiveness of manual lymphatic drainage (MLD) in patients with lipedema: A two-armed, randomized, controlled pragmatic trial Rainer Schneider, PhD RECON –Research and Consulting, Freiburg, Germany ABSTRACT Purpose : Although the exact prevalence of lipedema is unknown the number of women suffering from this condition is ever-growing. When treated conservatively, manual lymphatic drainage is regarded the gold standard. However, the rate of its effectiveness varies considerably with some women showing minimal to no improvement depending on severity of the disease and medical history. Methods : Thirty female patients diagnosed with lipedema stage 2 –3 referred to phy- siotherapeutic treatment were randomly allocated to either six treatments of MLD or to six treatments of combined MLD and vibrotherapy treatment. Outcome parameters were the volume of lipedema at four locations of either the lower ( n= 29) or the upper extremities ( n= 1), as well as quality of life. Findings : A very large superiority of effectiveness was found for the combined treatment. Reduction of the sizes of lipedema varied between 1.1 < d< 3.2. These patients ’quality of life was also considerably better ( d= 1.0). Conclusions : Combining MLD with vibrotherapy treatment drastically enhances the effectiveness of treating lipedema.ARTICLE HISTORY Received 7 August 2017 Revised 31 January 2018 Accepted 11 February 2018 KEYWORDS Lipedema; MLD; quality of life; shock wave vibrotherapy Introduction Lipedema is a chronic and progressive disease charac- terized by a disproportionate, symmetrical swelling of subcutaneous adipose tissue in the lower and, to a lesser degree, in the upper extremities of predominantly women. The disease is often painful and, if left untreated, may cause multiple secondary health pro- blems (e.g. mobility impairment). Both etiology and prevalence are largely unknown. However, lipedema usually develops early in puberty and is moderately correlated with hereditary hormonal disorders (Child et al, 2010 ). At later stages, some of the typical clinical features are: (1) small fatty nodules within the tissue; (2) bulges of fatty tissue above the knee; (3) swelling of the limbs throughout the day; and (4) sensitivity to pressure and easy bruising. Lipedema has received relativel y little scientific attention in the past. This is in part due to the fact that it is com- monly misdiagnosed as (bilateral) lymphedema, extreme cellulitis, or morbid obesity (Földi and Földi, 2010; Sze, Kemeny, Groma, and Szolnoky, 2014). Lipedema develops as a result of fatty tissue that triggers excessive compression of the superficial lymphatic collectors. In women suffering from lipedema, the inhibition of the lymphatic system ’s primary function (i.e. the removal of interstitial fluid) maytrigger the build-up of edema resulting in lipedema (Bae, Young-Joo, Jeong, and Kim, 2011). This condition aggra- vates over time because the ongoing strain on the lympha- tic system causes further functional and possibly structural deterioration. At advanced stages, lipedema progresses through the same stages as lymphedema and is therefore diagnosed accordingly (Morgan, 2008). It is important to note that lipedema is a fairly common disease that is often misconceived and thus misdiagnosed (Buck and Herbst, 2016). The exact prevalence of lipedema is unknown, but some epidemiologic studies report the following rates: 8% (Schubert and Viethen, 2016), 11% (Bae, Young-Joo, Jeong, and Kim, 2011), 15% (Herpertz, 1997), and 19% (Forner-Cordero, Szolnoky, Forner-Cordero, and Kemény, 2012) depending on the type of population investigated. In the recent past, research in this disease has been slowly growing, mainly because more and more women seek help. However, lipedema has as yet not been included in the International Classificatio no fD i s e a s e s( I C D )b yt h eW o r l d Health Organization. Active treatment of lipedema is recommended at early stages in order to improve the patient ’s long- term prognosis. Invasive treatment (e.g. liposuction) usually only becomes necessary at late stages when the CONTACT Rainer Schneider, PhD info@recon-freiburg.biz RECON - Research and Consulting, Unterer Mühlenweg 38B, Freiburg 79114 Germany.PHYSIOTHERAPY THEORY AND PRACTICE 2020, VOL. 36, NO. 1, 63 –70 https://doi.org/10.1080/09593985.2018.1479474 © 2018 Taylor & Francis physical and physiological impairments are too burden- ing

chunk_details.text: (Bae, Young-Joo, Jeong, and Kim, 2011), 15% (Herpertz, 1997), and 19% (Forner-Cordero, Szolnoky, Forner-Cordero, and Kemény, 2012) depending on the type of population investigated. In the recent past, research in this disease has been slowly growing, mainly because more and more women seek help. However, lipedema has as yet not been included in the International Classificatio no fD i s e a s e s( I C D )b yt h eW o r l d Health Organization. Active treatment of lipedema is recommended at early stages in order to improve the patient ’s long- term prognosis. Invasive treatment (e.g. liposuction) usually only becomes necessary at late stages when the CONTACT Rainer Schneider, PhD info@recon-freiburg.biz RECON - Research and Consulting, Unterer Mühlenweg 38B, Freiburg 79114 Germany.PHYSIOTHERAPY THEORY AND PRACTICE 2020, VOL. 36, NO. 1, 63 –70 https://doi.org/10.1080/09593985.2018.1479474 © 2018 Taylor & Francis physical and physiological impairments are too burden- ing or when the patient does not respond to conserva- tive treatment. Non-invasive/conservative treatment, on the other hand, aim at reducing edema, alleviating pain and hypersensitivity, and at increasing mobility when the patient still has a chance to reduce or main- tain the size of the affected body parts. When treated conservatively, complete decongestive therapy (CDT) is regarded the gold standard for treating both lymphe- dema and lipedema (Forner-Cordero, Szolnoky, Forner-Cordero, and Kemény, 2012 ; Mayrovitz, 2009 ). There is a general consensus among experts that CDT is safe and effective (Hinrichs et al, 2004; Koul, Dufan, Russel, and Cooke, 2007 ; Lasinski, 2002 ). Its main effects consist of: (1) reduction of swelling; (2) increase of lymph drainage from the congested areas; (3) reduc- tion of skin condition; (4) improvement of the func- tional status; and (5) improvement of quality of life (National Lymphedema Network, 2011 ). One integral part of CDT is manual lymph drainage (MLD). There are several “schools ”of MLD (e.g. Vodder, Földi, or Leduc), but they all share common features and do not significantly differ. At its core, MLD aims at stimulating superficial lymphatic vessels to remove exces- sive interstitial fluid. To do so, the therapist applies gentle, pumping, circular movements using low pressure. In addition to MLD ’s effects on the lymphatic system (e.g. transport capacity, fluid flow, and reduction of interstitial pressure/volume), it has positive effects on other physio- logical systems like the arterial system, the autonomic nervous system, or the urinary organs. Overall, the results from MLD are rather long-term requiring the patient to adhere to continuous treatment. However, the effects may vary depending on the type of location, comorbidity, and cause of the damage of the lymphatic system (Huang et al, 2013; Lasinski et al, 2012). In patients with lipedema, the lymphatic system appears to respond equally to MLD, but the reduction of subcutaneous fatty tissue responds, if at all, rather slowly. This is why different forms of compres- sion may be required (i.e. the second component of CDT), which may help to reduce excessive fat tissue (Child et al, 2010). T h el y m p h a t i cs y s t e mc a nb es t i m u l a t e dv i ad i f f e r e n t routes. Specifically, there are devices that produce vibra- tional impulses and waves, which aim at altering cell func- tioning. In the physiotherapeutic practice, such devices have mainly been applied to treat pain, but the body of evidence is rather inconclusi ve (Seco, Kovacs, and Urrutia, 2011). However, such devices typically use high and low energetic radial impulses and typically only apply local and punctual energy. The present study was conducted to test the effects of the transmission of mild, vertical, low –fre- quency impulses (Cellconnect Impulse).Method Study design and procedure The study was a two-armed, measurement repeated, randomized, and controlled pragmatic trial. The balanced random allocation sequence was generated using the software package SPSS 17. The random allo- cation sequence was implemented by a patient sequence list accessed by the therapists before each session. Treatment was performed by a female and a male physiotherapist. Both treated 15 patients each, and were randomly assigned to them. The therapists had a professional experience with lipedema of more than 25 years. To test the effectiveness of the interventions under routine practice conditions, patients were treated in accordance with German medical health care and pre- scription regulations, receiving six treatments of 30 minutes each. In the initial session, the physiothera- pist introduced the nature and purpose of the study. After patients gave their informed consent, baseline measures were taken. Then, participants were informed about the type of treatment they received according to the randomization protocol. Subsequently, the first treatment was applied. The therapeutic intervention comprised two treatments per week, totaling three weeks per patient. Upon the last
